title: Asymmetric thermal fields for excluding impurities in single crystal manufacturing device
abstract: Disclosed herein is a furnace and a method of growing a high temperature oxide crystal. The furnace includes a cylindrical furnace wall, an induction coil disposed within the cylindrical furnace wall, a quartz tube disposed within the induction coil, a refractory lining disposed within the quartz tube, and a crucible disposed within the refractory lining, the crucible including a melt therein. A lid placed on the crucible. An asymmetric configuration of at least one of the crucible, the refractory lining, the quartz tube, the induction coil and the lid within the cylindrical furnace wall creates a thermal gradient that causes a cold spot in the melt to migrate from a first location to a second location of the melt. A rod having a seed crystal at an end thereof is lowered into the crucible to draw a boule from the melt via the seed crystal from the first location.
claims:
Claims What Is Claimed Is: 1. A method of growing a high temperature oxide crystal, comprising: assembling a furnace including a cylindrical furnace wall, an induction coil disposed within the cylindrical furnace wall, a quartz tube disposed within the induction coil, a refractory lining disposed within the quartz tube, a crucible disposed within the refractory lining, the crucible including a melt therein, and a lid to cover the crucible; creating a thermal gradient in the melt by an asymmetric configuration of one the crucible, the refractory lining, the quartz tube, the induction coil, and the lid with respect to the cylindrical furnace wall to cause a cold spot in the melt to migrate from a first location to a second location of the melt; and drawing a boule from the first location away from the cold spot at the second location. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising creating the thermal gradient by moving a crucible central axis of the crucible to be eccentric from a furnace central axis of the furnace. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising creating the thermal gradient by the refractory lining having a first side that is thicker that a second side opposite the first side. 4. The method of claim 1, further comprising creating the thermal gradient by moving a quartz central axis of the quartz tube to be eccentric from a furnace central axis of the furnace. 5. The method of claim 1, further comprising creating the thermal gradient by moving a coil central axis of the induction coil to be eccentric from a furnace central axis of the furnace. 6. The method of claim 1, further comprising creating the thermal gradient by 

placing the lid asymmetrically with respect to the crucible. 7. The method of claim 1, further comprising creating the thermal gradient by introducing a hole in the refractory lining to one side of the crucible. 8. The method of claim 1, further comprising creating the thermal gradient by placing a cold finger against the quartz tube to one side of the crucible. 9. A furnace for growing a high temperature oxide crystal, comprising: a cylindrical furnace wall; an induction coil disposed within the cylindrical furnace wall; a quartz tube disposed within the induction coil; a refractory lining disposed within the quartz tube; a crucible disposed within the refractory lining, the crucible including a melt therein; a lid placed on the crucible, wherein an asymmetric configuration of at least one of the crucible, the refractory lining, the quartz tube, the induction coil and the lid within the cylindrical furnace wall creates a thermal gradient in the melt to cause a cold spot in the melt to migrate from a first location to a second location of the melt; and a rod having a seed crystal at an end thereof, wherein the rod is configured to draw a boule from the melt via the seed crystal from the first location. 10. The furnace of claim 9, wherein a crucible central axis of the crucible is eccentric from a furnace central axis of the furnace. 11. The furnace of claim 9, wherein the refractory lining has a first side that is thicker that a second side opposite the first side. 12. The furnace of claim 9, wherein a quartz central axis of the quartz tube is eccentric form a furnace central axis of the furnace. 13. The furnace of claim 9, wherein a coil central axis of the induction coil is eccentric to a furnace central axis of the furnace. 14. The furnace of claim 9, further comprising wherein the lid is placed asymmetrically with respect to the crucible. 15. The furnace of claim 9, further comprising a hole in the refractory lining to one side of the crucible that creates the thermal gradient. 16. The furnace of claim 9, further comprising a cold finger placed against the quartz tube to one side of the crucible that creates the thermal gradient.
